# Swift / SwiftUI conventions (production)

> **Project-scoped.** Loaded on-demand by **planning** (view/state/navigation
> architecture), **implementation** (idiomatic Swift), and **testing** (XCTest/Swift
> Testing structure). This is the iOS counterpart to `code-standards`; where a rule here
> is silent, `code-standards` (naming, function size, facade modules, no hardcoded
> secrets, input validation/output encoding) still applies. Fill every `<PLACEHOLDER>`.

## Targets & toolchain

- **Deployment target:** `<e.g. iOS 17.0>` — the version everything below assumes. Drop
  to 16/15 only with a recorded reason; note the API fallbacks per feature.
- **Swift language mode:** **Swift 6** with **strict concurrency checking** on (or Swift 5
  with `-strict-concurrency=complete` as a migration step). New code must be data-race-safe.
- **Xcode / SDK:** `<e.g. Xcode 16, iOS 18 SDK>`. UI built with **SwiftUI**; use UIKit only
  via `UIViewRepresentable` for a specific gap (camera, `PHPicker`, `WKWebView`), never as
  the default.

## Architecture — MV (Model–View) with Observation, not ceremonial MVVM

Apple's current guidance (post-Observation) is a **Model–View** pattern: SwiftUI views
observe `@Observable` model/state objects directly. Do **not** add a per-view `ViewModel`
class reflexively — that's MVVM cargo-culted from UIKit and adds a layer SwiftUI already
provides. Reach for a dedicated state object only when there is real presentation logic to
own or to share across views.

- **`@Observable` (Observation framework, iOS 17+)** for reference-type state/models. It
  tracks *which properties a view actually reads*, so it re-renders more precisely than the
  old `ObservableObject`/`@Published`. Use `@ObservableObject` + `@Published` **only** when
  targeting iOS < 17.
- **View-local value state** → `@State`. Pass mutable bindings down with `@Binding`; pass an
  observable object that a child mutates with `@Bindable`.
- **Shared/injected dependencies** → `@Environment` (see DI below).
- Keep **business/domain logic out of views** and out of `@Observable` UI state — it lives
  in plain Swift types (structs, services) that are unit-testable without SwiftUI.

```swift
@Observable final class CheckoutModel {          // owns presentation state + calls services
    var items: [LineItem] = []
    var isSubmitting = false
    private let orders: OrderService              // injected; a protocol, not a concrete type
    init(orders: OrderService) { self.orders = orders }
    func submit() async throws { isSubmitting = true; defer { isSubmitting = false }
        try await orders.place(items) }
}

struct CheckoutView: View {
    @State private var model: CheckoutModel        // view owns the model's lifetime
    var body: some View { /* reads model.items → re-renders only on that */ }
}
```

> For large apps with complex, testable side-effect flows, **The Composable Architecture
> (TCA)** is an acceptable alternative — but adopt it deliberately for the whole app, not
> per-screen. Record the choice in Stack notes; default is MV + `@Observable`.

## Navigation — value-driven `NavigationStack`

- Use **`NavigationStack`** with a **`NavigationPath`** (or a typed `[Route]`) and
  `.navigationDestination(for:)`. `NavigationView` is deprecated — do not use it.
- Model routes as an **enum** (`enum Route: Hashable { case detail(Item.ID); case settings }`)
  and drive navigation by appending to the path — this makes deep links and state
  restoration tractable.
- Tabs → `TabView`; modals → `.sheet`/`.fullScreenCover`; keep navigation state in one
  observable router when it's shared, not scattered across views.

## Concurrency — async/await, actors, `@MainActor`

- **All UI state mutation on the main actor.** Annotate view models `@MainActor` (or the
  specific methods); SwiftUI views are already main-actor.
- Use **`.task { }`** for view-lifetime async work (auto-cancelled on disappear) and
  **`.task(id:)`** to restart when a value changes. Prefer over `onAppear { Task { } }`.
- Do work off the main actor in **actors** or `nonisolated`/`Task.detached` functions;
  hop back by assigning to main-actor state. Types crossing concurrency boundaries must be
  **`Sendable`**.
- Cancel-aware: check `Task.isCancelled` / let cancellation propagate in long loops.
- Never block the main thread; no `DispatchSemaphore` waits on the main actor.

## Data layer

- **Persistence:** default **SwiftData** (`@Model`, `@Query`, `ModelContainer`) for iOS 17+;
  Core Data if the project needs its maturity/migrations or targets < 17. Record which.
  `<choose here>`
- **Networking:** `URLSession` with `async/await`; a typed client **facade** (protocol +
  concrete impl) so views/models depend on the protocol (`code-standards` facade rule).
  `Codable` models; decode off the main actor. No networking calls inside `body`.
- **Never** put secrets/API keys in the app bundle or source; use the Keychain for tokens
  and fetch config at runtime (`secrets-management`). A shipped IPA is fully inspectable.

## Dependency injection

- Prefer **initializer injection** of protocol dependencies (testable, explicit).
- For cross-cutting singletons, a custom `EnvironmentKey` + `@Environment`:

```swift
private struct OrderServiceKey: EnvironmentKey { static let defaultValue: OrderService = LiveOrderService() }
extension EnvironmentValues { var orderService: OrderService {
    get { self[OrderServiceKey.self] } set { self[OrderServiceKey.self] = newValue } } }
```

- Swap a **mock implementation** in previews/tests via `.environment(\.orderService, MockOrderService())`.

## Error handling

- Model errors as typed `Error` enums; surface user-facing failures through view state
  (an `error: AppError?` the view renders as an `.alert`), never a silent `try?`.
- Map lower-layer errors to a small app-facing set at the service facade — views don't see
  `URLError` directly. Mirrors the error-envelope discipline in `api-edge-conventions`.

## Performance

- **View identity & minimal invalidation:** small composable views; let `@Observable`'s
  property-level tracking do the work. Give `ForEach` **stable IDs** (`Identifiable`), never
  array indices for mutable collections.
- **Avoid `AnyView`** — it erases identity and defeats diffing; use `@ViewBuilder`,
  `Group`, or `if`/`switch` instead. Make expensive subviews `Equatable` (`EquatableView`)
  to skip redraws.
- **Lazy containers** (`LazyVStack`/`LazyVGrid`/`List`) for long/scrolling content; plain
  `VStack` builds all children eagerly.
- Use **`.drawingGroup()`** for heavy vector/blend rendering; profile with Instruments
  (SwiftUI + Time Profiler + Hangs) before optimizing. Move parsing/decoding off-main.

## Accessibility (required, not optional — see `apple-hig-compliance` for the full bar)

- Every meaningful control has an `.accessibilityLabel` (and `.accessibilityHint`/`.value`
  where useful); decorative images `.accessibilityHidden(true)`.
- Support **Dynamic Type** (text-style-relative fonts, `@ScaledMetric`) and check large
  sizes; support VoiceOver focus order (`.accessibilityElement(children:)`), Reduce Motion
  (`.accessibilityReduceMotion`), and sufficient contrast.

## Project layout & modularization

```
App/            // @main App, app-level composition root
Features/<X>/   // one folder per feature: Views, Model (@Observable), Services
DesignSystem/   // Color+Theme, Font+Theme, Metrics, reusable components (from claude-design-to-swiftui)
Core/           // networking facade, persistence, shared models, utilities
Resources/      // Assets.xcassets, fonts, localizations
Tests/          // unit + snapshot; UITests/ for XCUITest
```

- Split reusable layers (`DesignSystem`, `Core`) into **Swift Package Manager** local
  packages once the app grows — faster builds, enforced boundaries.
- Localize user-facing strings with **String Catalogs** (`.xcstrings`); never hardcode
  display text. Naming/size/comment rules follow `code-standards`.

## Testing stack (feeds the pipeline testing agent)

- **Unit:** **Swift Testing** (`import Testing`, `@Test`, `#expect`/`#require`, `@Suite`)
  on Xcode 16+; XCTest is still fine and required for UI tests. Test domain logic and
  models with dependencies mocked at the protocol boundary.
- **UI:** **XCUITest** (`XCUIApplication`) for critical end-to-end flows.
- **Snapshot / visual:** pointfreeco **swift-snapshot-testing** — the fidelity net for the
  Claude Design replication and the DS-plan design-review stage (`claude-design-to-swiftui`
  Step 5). Snapshot diffs are **advisory**, never a deploy gate.
- **Coverage:** enable code coverage in the test scheme (`xccov`/llvm-cov). **Honest status:**
  the pipeline's coverage runner is Python/JS-shaped today — surfacing a Swift coverage number
  requires the `xccov` adapter (iOS-plan Layer 3, not yet built), so until it lands the coverage
  figure is not auto-collected. That does **not** weaken the gate: **coverage % is surfaced, not
  gated** anyway (`test-conventions`), and the hard testing gates — **acceptance-criteria
  completeness** and **perf-pairing** — are language-agnostic (they count criteria, not lines)
  and carry regardless. Report coverage manually until the adapter exists.
- **Adversarial shapes still apply** — the IDOR/BOLA cross-owner-denial shape, constraint
  4xx-not-5xx, and injection-at-sink shapes from `test-conventions` are language-agnostic;
  reproduce them in Swift for any owner-scoped resource or boundary input.

## Non-negotiables (Swift echo of `code-standards`)

- No force-unwrap (`!`) or `try!` on fallible runtime values; use `guard let`/`if let` and
  typed errors. Force-unwrap only genuine programmer invariants, with a comment.
- No secrets in the bundle; tokens in Keychain; TLS for all network calls (ATS on).
- Validate/normalize external input (deep-link params, pasteboard, server payloads) before
  use; encode at the sink. `@MainActor` correctness over `@unchecked Sendable` shortcuts.
